title: README + user-guide reconciliation sweep

goal: Reconcile README.md feature matrix + docs/user-guide/ surface with the shipped Phase 5 capabilities. Audit for partial-truth documentation drift (same pattern as M31 T9). If docs/user-guide/README.md exists today, extend it to cross-link every shipped guide doc (agentic-loop / task-planner / copilot-service / copilot-ui plus the Phase 1-4 guides); if it doesn't exist, create it. Update README.md to reflect M35 exit test baseline (1162+ unit / 11 E2E specs / 12 Playwright cases) and Phase 5 feature coverage. Surface any drift where a shipped feature lacks a guide doc or a guide doc references a feature that changed name/shape across milestones. Pure documentation deliverable. Zero code / zero tests / zero IPC/bus/migrations.
acceptance: docs/user-guide/README.md exists at the user-guide root with an index linking every guide doc. Current guide docs inventory: agentic-loop.md / backup-and-restore.md / command-palette.md / configuring-providers.md / copilot-service.md / copilot-ui.md / getting-started.md / hiring-employees.md / keyboard-shortcuts.md / managing-projects.md / task-planner.md / using-the-vault.md (12 total as of M34 T11). If README.md exists already, audit it for completeness — every guide doc must have a one-line description entry in a scannable table or list grouped by Phase (Phase 1-2 basics / Phase 3 collaboration / Phase 4 safety / Phase 5 intelligence).

Repo root README.md feature matrix reconciled with shipped Phase 5 surface. Audit each feature callout: (a) is it still accurate vs the current code path; (b) is the linked docs/user-guide/*.md present; (c) do the test badge counts match the M35 exit baseline (1162+ unit / 11 E2E specs / 12 Playwright cases). Fix drift in place — don't leave a TODO.

Partial-truth audit pass modelled on M31 T9. Grep for stale references to Phase 4, v1.0.0 (where Phase 5 / v1.1.0 would be more correct in a user-facing doc), 'coming soon' / 'TBD' / 'draft' callouts that shipped features now cover. Surface findings and fix them — not tickets for later.

Verify every guide doc is cross-linked from at least one other place in the repo (README.md feature row or docs/user-guide/README.md index or another guide doc's 'See also' section). Orphaned guide docs are documentation debt.
Zero code changes, zero IPC, zero bus events, zero migrations, zero tests. Pure markdown deliverables.
Atomic commit: docs(m35): M35 T6 — README + user-guide reconciliation sweep.

docs/user-guide/ is NOT a new directory — 13 files already exist (12 guide docs + existing README.md verified via ls during T5 authoring). T6 is a reconciliation sweep, not a creation task. Audit first, modify in-place, avoid over-authoring.
Test baseline current at 1162 unit / 11 E2E specs / 12 Playwright cases (as of M35 T3). T6 must update README.md badge assertions to match. T8 version bump will pin this again in unit tests.
Phase 5 badge FROZEN at 'Phase 5' through v1.1.0 release. Do not update any visible badge copy to 'Phase 6' — that's a T8/T10 concern only if ever.

Architectural seams referenced in M35 T5 demo scenarios (pause-aware providerRouter wrapper, {rows,truncated} envelope, data-* selector surface, canned test-seam quartet, is_system filter-sweep, AbortController stop) are documented in retrospective §2 and design doc §13. README.md feature callouts should link to those authoritative sources rather than re-explaining them inline.

ABI state at start of T6: Node (post M35 T5 — but T5 was docs-only, so no rebuild ran). T6 is a pure docs task — no ABI rebuild required. Next rebuild needed at T8 unit-test addition (Node) or T10 full verification (both).
Commit cadence: single docs(m35) atomic commit for all markdown updates; single chore(loki) ledger commit. Both must land same session. Atomic + ledger cadence held through T1-T5; T6 preserves it.
Scope boundary for T6: NO new code, NO new tests, NO new IPC/bus/migrations. Audit-and-fix, not audit-and-file-tickets. Keep sweep scoped — a 20-minute sweep, not a 2-hour rewrite.

Surprise watch: docs/user-guide/README.md may already be thoroughly indexed (M34 T11 or earlier milestone shipped it). If so, T6 work focuses on the root README.md feature matrix + badge counts. Don't invent work that's already done.
